name: django-braces

metadata.name: django-braces
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation: Mixins to add easy functionality to Django class-based views, forms, and models.

classes.name: braces.views._ajax.JSONResponseMixin

methods.name: braces.views._ajax.JSONResponseMixin.get_content_type
documentation: Get the appropriate content type for the response

methods.name: braces.views._ajax.JSONResponseMixin.get_json_dumps_kwargs
documentation: Get kwargs for custom JSON compilation

methods.name: braces.views._ajax.JSONResponseMixin.get_json_encoder_class
documentation: Get the encoder class to use

methods.name: braces.views._ajax.JSONResponseMixin.render_json_response
documentation:
Limited serialization for shipping plain data.
Do not use for models or other complex objects.

methods.name: braces.views._ajax.JSONResponseMixin.render_json_object_response
documentation:
Serializes objects using Django's builtin JSON serializer. Additional
kwargs can be used the same way for django.core.serializers.serialize.

Basic serialized responses.

For anything more complicated than basic Python types or Django
models, please use something like django-rest-framework.

classes.name: braces.views._ajax.AjaxResponseMixin

methods.name: braces.views._ajax.AjaxResponseMixin.dispatch
documentation: Call the appropriate handler method

methods.name: braces.views._ajax.AjaxResponseMixin.get_ajax
documentation: Handle a GET request made with AJAX

methods.name: braces.views._ajax.AjaxResponseMixin.post_ajax
documentation: Handle a POST request made with AJAX

methods.name: braces.views._ajax.AjaxResponseMixin.put_ajax
documentation: Handle a PUT request made with AJAX

methods.name: braces.views._ajax.AjaxResponseMixin.delete_ajax
documentation: Handle a DELETE request made with AJAX

Mixin allows you to define alternative methods for ajax requests. Similar
to the normal get, post, and put methods, you can use get_ajax, post_ajax,
and put_ajax.

classes.name: braces.views._ajax.JsonRequestResponseMixin

methods.name: braces.views._ajax.JsonRequestResponseMixin.render_bad_request_response
documentation: Generate errors for bad content

methods.name: braces.views._ajax.JsonRequestResponseMixin.get_request_json
documentation: Get the JSON included in the body

methods.name: braces.views._ajax.JsonRequestResponseMixin.dispatch
documentation: Trigger the appropriate method

Attempt to parse the request body as JSON.

If successful, self.request_json will contain the deserialized object.
Otherwise, self.request_json will be None.

Set the attribute require_json to True to return a 400 "Bad Request" error
for requests that don't contain JSON.

Note: To allow public access to your view, you'll need to use the
csrf_exempt decorator or CsrfExemptMixin.

Example Usage:

    class SomeView(CsrfExemptMixin, JsonRequestResponseMixin):
        def post(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
            do_stuff_with_contents_of_request_json()
            return self.render_json_response(
                {'message': 'Thanks!'})

classes.name: braces.views._ajax.JSONRequestResponseMixin
documentation: Convenience alias

classes.name: braces.views._forms.CsrfExemptMixin
documentation:
Exempts the view from CSRF requirements.

NOTE:
    This should be the left-most mixin of a view.

classes.name: braces.views._forms.UserFormKwargsMixin
documentation:
Automatically include `request.user` in form kwargs.

## Note
You will need to handle the `user` kwarg in your form. Usually
this means `user = kwargs.pop("user")` in your form's `__init__`.

classes.name: braces.views._forms.SuccessURLRedirectListMixin

class_variables.name: braces.views._forms.SuccessURLRedirectListMixin.success_list_url
documentation: Default the success url to none

Automatically reverses `success_list_url` and returns that as
the `success_url` for a form view.

This is meant to redirect to a view without arguments. If you need
to include arguments to `reverse`, you can omit this mixin.

classes.name: braces.views._forms._MessageAPIWrapper
documentation:
Wrapper for the django.contrib.messages.api module.
Automatically pass a request object as the first parameter of
message function calls.

classes.name: braces.views._forms._MessageDescriptor
documentation:
A descriptor that binds the _MessageAPIWrapper to the view's
request.

classes.name: braces.views._forms.MessageMixin
documentation:
Add a `messages` attribute on the view instance that wraps
`django.contrib.messages`, automatically passing the current
request object.

classes.name: braces.views._forms.FormValidMessageMixin

methods.name: braces.views._forms.FormValidMessageMixin.get_form_valid_message
documentation: Validate that form_valid_message is set correctly

methods.name: braces.views._forms.FormValidMessageMixin.form_valid
documentation: Set the "form valid" message for standard form validation

methods.name: braces.views._forms.FormValidMessageMixin.delete
documentation: Set the "form valid" message for delete form validation

class_variables.name: braces.views._forms.FormValidMessageMixin.form_valid_message
documentation: Default to None

Set a string to be sent via Django's messages framework when a form
passes validation.

classes.name: braces.views._forms.FormInvalidMessageMixin

methods.name: braces.views._forms.FormInvalidMessageMixin.get_form_invalid_message
documentation: Validate that form_invalid_message is set correctly.

methods.name: braces.views._forms.FormInvalidMessageMixin.form_invalid
documentation: Set the "form invalid" message for standard form validation

Set a string to be sent via Django's messages framework when a form
fails validation.

classes.name: braces.views._forms.FormMessagesMixin
documentation: Set messages to be sent whether a form is valid or invalid.

modules.name: braces
documentation:
django-braces mixins library
----------------------------

Mixins to make Django's generic class-based views simpler.

:copyright: (c) 2013 by Kenneth Love and Chris Jones
:license: BSD 3-clause. See LICENSE for more details

classes.name: braces.forms.UserKwargModelFormMixin

methods.name: braces.forms.UserKwargModelFormMixin.__init__
documentation: Remove the user from **kwargs and assign it on the object

Generic model form mixin for popping user out of the kwargs and
attaching it to the instance.

This mixin must precede forms.ModelForm/forms.Form. The form is not
expecting these kwargs to be passed in, so they must be popped off before
anything else is done.

Augments context with:

> - `ordering` - order of ordering, either `asc` or `desc`

Augments `QuerySet` with order_by statement if possible

```
param QuerySet queryset

QuerySet to order_by

return

QuerySet
```

documentation: Update the context to include the static content

classes.name: braces.views._other.CanonicalSlugDetailMixin

methods.name: braces.views._other.CanonicalSlugDetailMixin.dispatch
documentation:
Redirect to the appropriate URL if necessary.
Otherwise, trigger HTTP-method-appropriate handler.

methods.name: braces.views._other.CanonicalSlugDetailMixin.get_canonical_slug
documentation: Provide a method to return the correct slug for this object.

Enforce a canonical slug in the URL.

If a URL takes a object's pk and slug as arguments and the slug URL
argument does not equal the object's canonical slug, this mixin will
redirect to the URL containing the canonical slug.

classes.name: braces.views._other.AllVerbsMixin

methods.name: braces.views._other.AllVerbsMixin.dispatch
documentation: Call the all handler

Call a single method for all HTTP verbs.

The name of the method should be specified using the class attribute
`all_handler`. The default value of this attribute is 'all'.

classes.name: braces.views._other.HeaderMixin

methods.name: braces.views._other.HeaderMixin.dispatch
documentation:
Override this method to customize the way additional headers are
retrieved. It is mandatory that the returned value supports the
`.items()` method.

Add extra HTTP headers to a response by specifying them in the
`headers` attribute or by overriding the `get_headers()` method.

classes.name: braces.views._other.CacheControlMixin

methods.name: braces.views._other.CacheControlMixin.get_cachecontrol_options
documentation: Compile a dictionary of selected cache options

methods.name: braces.views._other.CacheControlMixin.as_view
documentation: Wrap the view with appropriate cache controls

Mixin that allows setting Cache-Control options.

Specify Cache-Control options as class attributes on the view class.

Cache-Control directive explanations:
<http://condor.depaul.edu/dmumaugh/readings/handouts/SE435/HTTP/node24.html>

Django's `django.views.decorators.cache.cache_control` options:
<https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/dev/topics/cache/#controlling-cache-using-other-headers>

classes.name: braces.views._other.NeverCacheMixin

methods.name: braces.views._other.NeverCacheMixin.as_view
documentation: Wrap the view with the `never_cache` decorator.

Mixin that applies Django's `never_cache` view decorator to prevent
upstream HTTP-based caching.

methods.name: braces.views._access.AccessMixin.get_login_url
documentation: Override this method to customize the login_url.

methods.name: braces.views._access.AccessMixin.get_redirect_field_name
documentation: Override this method to customize the redirect_field_name.

methods.name: braces.views._access.AccessMixin.handle_no_permission
documentation: What should happen if the user doesn't have permission?

methods.name: braces.views._access.AccessMixin.no_permissions_fail
documentation:
Called when the user has no permissions and no exception was raised.
This should only return a valid HTTP response.

By default we redirect to login.

class_variables.name: braces.views._access.AccessMixin.redirect_field_name
documentation: Set by django.contrib.auth
Base access mixin. All other access mixins should extend this one.

classes.name: braces.views._access.LoginRequiredMixin

methods.name: braces.views._access.LoginRequiredMixin.dispatch
documentation: Call the appropriate method after checking authentication

Requires the user to be authenticated.

NOTE:
    This should be the left-most mixin of a view, except when
    combined with CsrfExemptMixin - which in that case should
    be the left-most mixin.

classes.name: braces.views._access.AnonymousRequiredMixin

methods.name: braces.views._access.AnonymousRequiredMixin.dispatch
documentation: Call the appropriate handler after guaranteeing anonymity

methods.name: braces.views._access.AnonymousRequiredMixin.get_authenticated_redirect_url
documentation: Return the reversed authenticated redirect url.

Requires the user to be unauthenticated.

NOTE:
    This should be the left-most mixin of a view.

## Example Usage

    class SomeView(AnonymousRequiredMixin, ListView):
        ...
        # required
        authenticated_redirect_url = "/accounts/profile/"
        ...

classes.name: braces.views._access.PermissionRequiredMixin

methods.name: braces.views._access.PermissionRequiredMixin.get_permission_required
documentation:
Get the required permissions and return them.

Override this to allow for custom permission_required values.

methods.name: braces.views._access.PermissionRequiredMixin.check_permissions
documentation: Returns whether or not the user has permissions

methods.name: braces.views._access.PermissionRequiredMixin.dispatch
documentation:
Check to see if the user in the request has the required
permission.

class_variables.name: braces.views._access.PermissionRequiredMixin.permission_required
documentation: No permissions are required by default

The request users must have certain permission(s)

## Attributes

`permission_required` - the permission to check for.
`login_url` - the login url of site
`redirect_field_name` - defaults to "next"
`raise_exception` - defaults to False - raise 403 if set to True

## Example Usage

    class SomeView(PermissionRequiredMixin, ListView):
        ...
        # required
        permission_required = "app.permission"

        # optional
        login_url = "/signup/"
        redirect_field_name = "hollaback"
        raise_exception = True
        ...

classes.name: braces.views._access.MultiplePermissionsRequiredMixin

methods.name: braces.views._access.MultiplePermissionsRequiredMixin.get_permission_required
documentation: Get which permission is required

methods.name: braces.views._access.MultiplePermissionsRequiredMixin.check_permissions
documentation: Get the permissions, both all and any.

methods.name: braces.views._access.MultiplePermissionsRequiredMixin._check_permissions_attr
documentation: Check permissions attribute is set and that it is a dict.

methods.name: braces.views._access.MultiplePermissionsRequiredMixin._check_permissions_keys_set
documentation:
Check to make sure the keys `any` or `all` are not both blank.
If both are blank either an empty dict came in or the wrong keys
came in. Both are invalid and should raise an exception.

methods.name: braces.views._access.MultiplePermissionsRequiredMixin._check_perms_keys
documentation:
If the permissions list/tuple passed in is set, check to make
sure that it is of the type list or tuple.

class_variables.name: braces.views._access.MultiplePermissionsRequiredMixin.permissions
documentation: Default required perms to none

View mixin which allows you to specify two types of permission
requirements. The `permissions` attribute must be a dict which
specifies two keys, `all` and `any`. You can use either one on
its own or combine them. The value of each key is required to be a
list or tuple of permissions. The standard Django permissions
style is not strictly enforced. If you have created your own
permissions in a different format, they should still work.

By specifying the `all` key, the user must have all of
the permissions in the list.

By specifying the `any` key , the user must have at least one of the
permissions in the list.

Class Settings
    `permissions` - This is required to be a dict with one or both
        keys of `all` and `any` containing a list or tuple of
        permissions.
    `login_url` - the login url of site
    `redirect_field_name` - defaults to "next"
    `raise_exception` - defaults to False - raise 403 if set to True

Example Usage
    class SomeView(MultiplePermissionsRequiredMixin, ListView):
        ...
        #required
        permissions = {
            "all": ("blog.add_post", "blog.change_post"),
            "any": ("blog.delete_post", "user.change_user")
        }

        #optional
        login_url = "/signup/"
        redirect_field_name = "hollaback"
        raise_exception = True

methods.name: braces.views._access.GroupRequiredMixin.get_group_required
documentation: Get which group's membership is required

methods.name: braces.views._access.GroupRequiredMixin.check_membership
documentation:
Check for user's membership in required groups. Superusers are
automatically members

methods.name: braces.views._access.GroupRequiredMixin.dispatch
documentation: Call the appropriate handler if the user is a group member

classes.name: braces.views._access.UserPassesTestMixin

methods.name: braces.views._access.UserPassesTestMixin.test_func
documentation: The function to test the user with

methods.name: braces.views._access.UserPassesTestMixin.get_test_func
documentation: Get the test function

methods.name: braces.views._access.UserPassesTestMixin.dispatch
documentation: Call the appropriate handler if the users passes the test

User must pass a test before being allowed access to the view.

Class Settings
    `test_func` - This is required to be a method that takes user
        instance and return True or False after checking conditions.
    `login_url` - the login url of site
    `redirect_field_name` - defaults to "next"
    `raise_exception` - defaults to False - raise 403 if set to True

classes.name: braces.views._access.SuperuserRequiredMixin

methods.name: braces.views._access.SuperuserRequiredMixin.dispatch
documentation: Call the appropriate handler if the user is a superuser
Require users to be superusers to access the view.

classes.name: braces.views._access.StaffuserRequiredMixin

methods.name: braces.views._access.StaffuserRequiredMixin.dispatch
documentation: Call the appropriate handler if the user is a staff member
Require users to be marked as staff to access the view.

classes.name: braces.views._access.SSLRequiredMixin

methods.name: braces.views._access.SSLRequiredMixin.dispatch
documentation: Call the appropriate handler if the connection is secure

methods.name: braces.views._access.SSLRequiredMixin._build_https_url

classes.name: braces.views._access.RecentLoginRequiredMixin

methods.name: braces.views._access.RecentLoginRequiredMixin.dispatch
documentation: Call the appropriate method if the user's login is recent

class_variables.name: braces.views._access.RecentLoginRequiredMixin.max_last_login_delta
documentation: Defaults to 30 minutes
Require the user to have logged in within a number of seconds.
